title: Puerto Banus Speedboat Rental by the Hour: Prices and Tips for 2026
html:
Renting a speedboat by the hour in Puerto Banus offers a flexible way to explore the Costa del Sol coastline, with half-day trips costing from €300 to €600 for a 28ft dayboat, and you can compare verified options on BoatHire24.

How much does it cost to rent a speedboat hourly in Puerto Banus?

Hourly speedboat rentals in Puerto Banus range from €150 to €250 per hour for a 28ft boat, with a typical minimum of three hours. Prices depend on season, boat size, and whether you hire a skipper. For the best rates, you can book a charter online through platforms that list multiple operators. You can book a charter online in a few minutes.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Puerto Banus marina is the principal departure point, located in the heart of Marbella's luxury resort area. Nearby marinas include Puerto José Banus itself and the smaller Club de Mar in Marbella town. Popular cruising grounds include the bay of Marbella, the beach of San Pedro de Alcántara, and the cove of Cabo Pino. Further west, the port of Estepona offers an alternative stop for a longer day out. The Gibraltar Strait is visible on clear days but is over 40 nautical miles away. One option worth a look is the Limpopo for hire.

Which boat to rent

  1. Bowrider speedboat: ideal for couples or small groups, seating up to 6 people, from €200 per hour.
  2. Day cruiser (e.g., Axopar 28): versatile for coastal hopping, up to 8 guests, from €250 per hour. For a specific example, see the Limpopo for hire, which is a well-maintained Axopar 28.
  3. Rigid inflatable (RIB): perfect for watersports and fishing, for up to 8 guests, from €150 per hour.
  4. Motor yacht (e.g., Sunseeker Predator): for a premium experience, up to 12 guests, from €500 per hour.

How much does a full day cost?

For longer durations, operators often offer half-day and full-day packages. A four-hour morning charter for a small motorboat starts around €600, while a full day (eight hours) can reach €1,200 for a 30ft model. Catamaran day charters, such as a Lagoon 42, cost from €1,500 for a full day, including crew. Crewed superyacht charters (e.g., a 50ft Azimut) exceed €3,000 per day. Use the boat search on BoatHire24 to compare prices and availability for your dates. You can also see more at check availability.

  • Shared tour: from €80 per person for 2 hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hrs): from €300.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hrs): from €1,500.
  • Crewed yacht full-day (with skipper): from €800.

Do you need a licence to drive a speedboat in Puerto Banus?

Yes, to skipper a speedboat independently in Spain you need a valid recreational boating licence, but for hourly rentals a licensed skipper is typically included. Most hourly rental packages in Puerto Banus come with a professional captain, so no personal licence is required. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking. High season is June through September, when the Levante wind can pick up in the afternoon, making morning trips more comfortable. To see how booking works, visit the boat search.

For the calmest sea conditions, book a morning slot before the Levante wind rises, typically after 2pm in summer.

At a glance

  • Price band: €150, €500 per hour for private speedboats.
  • Peak months: June to September.
  • Trip length: 2 to 8 hours, with 3, 4 hour minimums common.
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests on larger boats.
  • Currency: all prices in EUR.

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can you rent a boat in Puerto Banus without a skipper?

Some boats are available for bareboat charter if you hold a Spanish-recognised licence, but most hourly rentals include a skipper by default.

What is the best time of year to rent a speedboat in Puerto Banus?

The best time is May to October, with July and August offering the warmest weather but also higher prices and busier marinas.

How far can you go in a three-hour speedboat rental from Puerto Banus?

From Puerto Banus, you can easily reach Marbella's coast to the east or Estepona to the west, covering around 15, 20 nautical miles in a three-hour trip at cruising speed.
